payload.summary: RTL decomposition for a 3-stage single-issue RV32EC_Zmmul CPU with fetch, decode/control, execute/writeback, RV32E register file, CSR/trap control, load/store handshake, multiply support, and boot ROM loader assist including observable integration testcases.

rv32ec_zmmul_cpu_top.description: Top-level integration of the RV32EC_Zmmul CPU. It sequences reset/boot behavior, instruction fetch, decode, execute/writeback, load/store transactions, trap/interrupt routing, multiply stalls, and boot-loader fail-stop behavior. Testcases are observable only through top-level interfaces such as fetch/data handshakes, redirect/trap behavior, boot completion, and fail-stop.

outputs.name: fail_stop
outputs.description: Terminal boot or fatal failure state

outputs.name: trap_pc
outputs.description: Redirect PC used for trap/interrupt entry

inferred_testcases: RESET_FETCH_START: rst_n=0 then rst_n=1, boot_mode_spi=0, instr_rvalid=0, ext_irq=0 → instr_req=1, instr_addr=reset_vector, boot_done=0, fail_stop=0

SEQUENTIAL_16BIT_FETCH: rst_n=1, boot_mode_spi=0, instr_rvalid=1, instr_rdata=0x00000001 (16-bit compressed in low halfword), no redirects/traps → next observable instr_req=1, next instr_addr=PC+2, trap_taken=0, fail_stop=0

SEQUENTIAL_32BIT_FETCH: rst_n=1, boot_mode_spi=0, instr_rvalid=1, instr_rdata=0x00000033 (32-bit base instruction low bits[1:0]=11), no redirects/traps → next observable instr_req=1, next instr_addr=PC+4, trap_taken=0, fail_stop=0
BRANCH_TAKEN_REDIRECT: provide a taken branch instruction stream with operands causing taken result and valid fetch responses → observable next instr_addr=branch_target, trap_taken=0, fail_stop=0
JAL_REDIRECT: provide JAL instruction fetch response encoding target offset with valid execution progress → observable next instr_addr=jal_target, trap_taken=0, fail_stop=0
JALR_REDIRECT: provide JALR instruction plus source operand via preceding setup and valid execution progress → observable next instr_addr=jalr_target_aligned, trap_taken=0, fail_stop=0
LOAD_HANDSHAKE: execute a load instruction sequence, data_gnt=1, data_rvalid=1, data_rdata=0x12345678 → data_req=1, data_we=0, data_addr=effective_address, no fail_stop, no unexpected trap
STORE_HANDSHAKE: execute a store instruction sequence, data_gnt=1 → data_req=1, data_we=1, data_addr=effective_address, data_wdata=store_data, data_be=expected_byte_enables, no unexpected trap

LOAD_WAIT_STATE_STALL: execute a load instruction sequence with data_gnt=1 and delayed data_rvalid=0 for several cycles → data_req remains asserted/pending behavior observable, no new completed external redirect/trap until response, fail_stop=0
MUL_INTEGRATION_NO_TRAP: execute a MUL instruction sequence with valid instruction flow → no trap_taken, no fail_stop, front-end fetch progression resumes after multiply latency
DIV_ILLEGAL_TRAP: present unsupported DIV opcode in instruction stream with instr_rvalid=1 → trap_taken=1, trap_pc=mtvec, fail_stop=0
REM_ILLEGAL_TRAP: present unsupported REM opcode in instruction stream with instr_rvalid=1 → trap_taken=1, trap_pc=mtvec, fail_stop=0
ILLEGAL_X16_REG_TRAP: present instruction encoding naming x16 in rd/rs1/rs2 with instr_rvalid=1 → trap_taken=1, trap_pc=mtvec, fail_stop=0
ILLEGAL_UNKNOWN_EXTENSION_TRAP: present unsupported extension opcode with instr_rvalid=1 → trap_taken=1, trap_pc=mtvec, fail_stop=0
EXTERNAL_INTERRUPT_ENTRY: ext_irq=1 while core running with traps enabled context → trap_taken=1, trap_pc=mtvec, fail_stop=0
MRET_RETURN_FLOW: execute trap handler stream containing MRET after prior trap entry → observable fetch redirect to saved mepc, trap_taken deasserts after return flow, fail_stop=0

SPI_BOOT_SUCCESS: rst_n=1, boot_mode_spi=1, spi_rx_valid sequence provides valid header/version/program_size/start address and image data, spi_error=0 → spi_tx_req asserted during load, boot_done=1, fail_stop=0
SPI_BOOT_HEADER_MALFORMED_FAIL: rst_n=1, boot_mode_spi=1, spi_rx_valid=1, spi_rx_data=malformed_header, spi_error=0 → fail_stop=1, boot_done=0
SPI_BOOT_SIZE_VIOLATION_FAIL: rst_n=1, boot_mode_spi=1, spi_rx_valid sequence with program_size>=16384 or out-of-range start address → fail_stop=1, boot_done=0
SPI_BOOT_SPI_ERROR_FAIL: rst_n=1, boot_mode_spi=1, spi_error=1 during boot transaction → fail_stop=1, boot_done=0

fetch_unit.description: Maintains PC, issues instruction fetch requests, selects next PC based on trap/MRET/branch/ sequential priority, and advances by 2 or 4 depending on compressed/uncompressed instruction length.
inferred_testcases: RESET_VECTOR_LOAD: rst_n=0 then rst_n=1, reset_vector_i=0x00000100, all redirects=0, stall_i=0 → pc_o=0x00000100, instr_req_o=1, instr_addr_o=0x00000100
SEQUENTIAL_PC_PLUS_2: rst_n=1, pc starts 0x00000100, instr_is_16b_i=1, all redirects=0, stall_i=0 → next pc_o=0x00000102, instr_addr_o=0x00000102
SEQUENTIAL_PC_PLUS_4: rst_n=1, pc starts 0x00000100, instr_is_16b_i=0, all redirects=0, stall_i=0 → next pc_o=0x00000104, instr_addr_o=0x00000104
BRANCH_REDIRECT: trap_redirect_valid_i=0, mret_redirect_valid_i=0, branch_redirect_valid_i=1, branch_redirect_pc_i=0x00000200, stall_i=0 → pc_o=0x00000200, instr_addr_o=0x00000200

MRET_REDIRECT_PRIORITY_OVER_BRANCH: mret_redirect_valid_i=1, mret_redirect_pc_i=0x00000300, branch_redirect_valid_i=1, branch_redirect_pc_i=0x00000200, trap_redirect_valid_i=0, stall_i=0 → pc_o=0x00000300, instr_addr_o=0x00000300

TRAP_REDIRECT_HIGHEST_PRIORITY: trap_redirect_valid_i=1, trap_redirect_pc_i=0x00000400, mret_redirect_valid_i=1, mret_redirect_pc_i=0x00000300, branch_redirect_valid_i=1, branch_redirect_pc_i=0x00000200, stall_i=0 → pc_o=0x00000400, instr_addr_o=0x00000400
STALL_HOLDS_PC: pc starts 0x00000100, stall_i=1, all redirects=0, instr_is_16b_i=0 → pc_o remains 0x00000100, instr_addr_o=0x00000100

decode_control_unit.description: Decodes RV32E, compressed C, Zmmul, branch/jump, load/store, CSR, and trap-return instructions; generates control outputs; flags illegal divide/rem, unsupported extensions, and any use of x16..x31.

inputs.name: instr_i
inputs.description: Instruction word after decompression if needed

inferred_testcases: ADD_DECODE: instr_i=0x003100B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → rs1_o=2, rs2_o=3, rd_o=1, alu_op_o=ADD, is_mul_o=0, illegal_o=0
SUB_DECODE: instr_i=0x403100B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → rs1_o=2, rs2_o=3, rd_o=1, alu_op_o=SUB, illegal_o=0
AND_DECODE: instr_i=0x003170B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → alu_op_o=AND, illegal_o=0
OR_DECODE: instr_i=0x003160B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → alu_op_o=OR, illegal_o=0
XOR_DECODE: instr_i=0x003140B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → alu_op_o=XOR, illegal_o=0
SLL_DECODE: instr_i=0x003110B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → alu_op_o=SLL, illegal_o=0
SRL_DECODE: instr_i=0x003150B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → alu_op_o=SRL, illegal_o=0
SRA_DECODE: instr_i=0x403150B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → alu_op_o=SRA, illegal_o=0
SLT_DECODE: instr_i=0x003120B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → alu_op_o=SLT, illegal_o=0
SLTU_DECODE: instr_i=0x003130B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → alu_op_o=SLTU, illegal_o=0
ADDI_DECODE: instr_i=0x00510093, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → rs1_o=2, rd_o=1, imm_sel_o=I, alu_op_o=ADD, illegal_o=0
LOAD_DECODE: instr_i=0x00012083, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → is_load_o=1, imm_sel_o=I, illegal_o=0
STORE_DECODE: instr_i=0x00112023, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → is_store_o=1, imm_sel_o=S, illegal_o=0
BRANCH_DECODE: instr_i=0x00208863, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → is_branch_o=1, imm_sel_o=B, illegal_o=0
JAL_DECODE: instr_i=0x008000EF, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → is_jump_o=1, imm_sel_o=J, illegal_o=0
JALR_DECODE: instr_i=0x000100E7, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → is_jump_o=1, imm_sel_o=I, illegal_o=0
LUI_DECODE: instr_i=0x123450B7, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → rd_o=1, imm_sel_o=U, illegal_o=0
AUIPC_DECODE: instr_i=0x12345097, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → rd_o=1, imm_sel_o=U, illegal_o=0
MUL_DECODE: instr_i=0x023100B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → is_mul_o=1, rd_o=1, rs1_o=2, rs2_o=3, illegal_o=0
CSR_DECODE: instr_i=0x300020F3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → is_csr_o=1, illegal_o=0
MRET_DECODE: instr_i=0x30200073, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → is_mret_o=1, illegal_o=0
DIV_ILLEGAL_DECODE: instr_i=0x023140B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → illegal_o=1
REM_ILLEGAL_DECODE: instr_i=0x023160B3, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → illegal_o=1
X16_ILLEGAL_RD_DECODE: instr_i=0x00310833, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → illegal_o=1
UNKNOWN_EXTENSION_ILLEGAL_DECODE: instr_i=0x0000100F, instr_is_compressed_i=0 → illegal_o=1

immediate_generator.description: Generates sign-extended or zero-extended immediates for I, S, B, U, J, and compressed instruction forms.
inferred_testcases: IMM_I_POSITIVE: instr_i=0x00510093, imm_sel_i=I → imm_o=0x00000005
IMM_I_NEGATIVE: instr_i=0xFFF10093, imm_sel_i=I → imm_o=0xFFFFFFFF
IMM_S: instr_i=0xFE112E23, imm_sel_i=S → imm_o=0xFFFFFFFC
IMM_B: instr_i=0x00208863, imm_sel_i=B → imm_o=0x00000010
IMM_U: instr_i=0x123450B7, imm_sel_i=U → imm_o=0x12345000
IMM_J: instr_i=0x008000EF, imm_sel_i=J → imm_o=0x00000008

compressed_decoder.description: Decodes supported compressed RV32C/RV32EC instruction encodings and expands them to equivalent internal 32-bit operations or direct control indications; flags unsupported compressed encodings as illegal.
inferred_testcases: C_MV_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x818A → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_ADDI_or_MV_form
C_ADD_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x918E → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_ADD_form
C_ANDI_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x888D → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_ANDI_form
C_SRLI_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x8005 → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_SRLI_form
C_SRAI_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x8405 → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_SRAI_form
C_SUB_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x8C01 → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_SUB_form
C_XOR_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x8C21 → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_XOR_form
C_OR_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x8C41 → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_OR_form
C_AND_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x8C61 → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=equivalent_AND_form
C_EBREAK_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x9002 → is_compressed_o=1, illegal_o=0, expanded_instr_o=0x00100073
C_ILLEGAL_DECODE: c_instr_i=0x0000 → illegal_o=1

alu32.description: Performs 32-bit arithmetic, logical, shift, and compare operations used by execute stage.
inferred_testcases: ADD: a_i=0x00000005 b_i=0x00000003 alu_op_i=ADD → result_o=0x00000008
SUB: a_i=0x00000005 b_i=0x00000003 alu_op_i=SUB → result_o=0x00000002
AND: a_i=0x0000000F b_i=0x00000033 alu_op_i=AND → result_o=0x00000003
OR: a_i=0x0000000F b_i=0x00000030 alu_op_i=OR → result_o=0x0000003F
XOR: a_i=0x0000000F b_i=0x00000033 alu_op_i=XOR → result_o=0x0000003C
SLL: a_i=0x00000001 b_i=0x00000004 alu_op_i=SLL → result_o=0x00000010
SRL: a_i=0x80000000 b_i=0x00000004 alu_op_i=SRL → result_o=0x08000000
SRA: a_i=0x80000000 b_i=0x00000004 alu_op_i=SRA → result_o=0xF8000000
SLT_TRUE: a_i=0xFFFFFFFF b_i=0x00000001 alu_op_i=SLT → result_o=0x00000001
SLT_FALSE: a_i=0x00000002 b_i=0x00000001 alu_op_i=SLT → result_o=0x00000000
SLTU_TRUE: a_i=0x00000001 b_i=0xFFFFFFFF alu_op_i=SLTU → result_o=0x00000001
SLTU_FALSE: a_i=0xFFFFFFFF b_i=0x00000001 alu_op_i=SLTU → result_o=0x00000000

branch_jump_unit.description: Evaluates branch conditions and computes branch/jump/JALR targets, including JALR LSB clearing.

outputs.name: link_addr_o
outputs.description: PC+2/4 link value as selected externally

inferred_testcases: BEQ_TAKEN: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0x00000005 rs2_i=0x00000005 imm_i=0x00000010 branch_funct3_i=000 is_branch_i=1 is_jal_i=0 is_jalr_i=0 → take_o=1, target_o=0x00000110
BEQ_NOT_TAKEN: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0x00000005 rs2_i=0x00000006 imm_i=0x00000010 branch_funct3_i=000 is_branch_i=1 is_jal_i=0 is_jalr_i=0 → take_o=0
BNE_TAKEN: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0x00000005 rs2_i=0x00000006 imm_i=0x00000010 branch_funct3_i=001 is_branch_i=1 → take_o=1, target_o=0x00000110
BLT_TAKEN: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0xFFFFFFFF rs2_i=0x00000001 imm_i=0x00000010 branch_funct3_i=100 is_branch_i=1 → take_o=1, target_o=0x00000110
BGE_TAKEN: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0x00000002 rs2_i=0x00000001 imm_i=0x00000010 branch_funct3_i=101 is_branch_i=1 → take_o=1, target_o=0x00000110
BLTU_TAKEN: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0x00000001 rs2_i=0xFFFFFFFF imm_i=0x00000010 branch_funct3_i=110 is_branch_i=1 → take_o=1, target_o=0x00000110
BGEU_TAKEN: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0xFFFFFFFF rs2_i=0x00000001 imm_i=0x00000010 branch_funct3_i=111 is_branch_i=1 → take_o=1, target_o=0x00000110
JAL_TAKEN: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0x00000000 rs2_i=0x00000000 imm_i=0x00000020 is_branch_i=0 is_jal_i=1 is_jalr_i=0 → take_o=1, target_o=0x00000120
JALR_TAKEN_ALIGNED: pc_i=0x00000100 rs1_i=0x00000203 rs2_i=0x00000000 imm_i=0x00000004 is_branch_i=0 is_jal_i=0 is_jalr_i=1 → take_o=1, target_o=0x00000206

multiplier_unit.description: Implements RV32M multiply subset required by Zmmul. Supports handshake with start/busy/done and returns lower 32-bit product for MUL.
inferred_testcases: MUL_POSITIVE: start_i=1 a_i=0x00000005 b_i=0x00000003 → busy_o=1 during operation, done_o=1 on completion, result_o=0x0000000F
MUL_ZERO: start_i=1 a_i=0x00000000 b_i=0x12345678 → done_o=1 on completion, result_o=0x00000000
MUL_NEGATIVE_TIMES_POSITIVE: start_i=1 a_i=0xFFFFFFFF b_i=0x00000002 → done_o=1 on completion, result_o=0xFFFFFFFE
MUL_OVERFLOW_LOW32: start_i=1 a_i=0x80000000 b_i=0x00000002 → done_o=1 on completion, result_o=0x00000000
RESET_IDLE: rst_n=0 → busy_o=0, done_o=0

execute_writeback_unit.description: Consumes decoded control and operand data, selects ALU/multiply/load/CSR result, generates register writeback and redirect requests, and coordinates stalls for multicycle operations.
inferred_testcases: ALU_WRITEBACK: rs1_val_i=0x00000005 rs2_val_i=0x00000003 alu_op_i=ADD is_alu_i=1 is_mul_i=0 is_load_i=0 is_csr_i=0 rd_i=1 → wb_we_o=1, wb_rd_o=1, wb_data_o=0x00000008, stall_req_o=0
MUL_START_AND_STALL: is_alu_i=0 is_mul_i=1 rd_i=1 mul_done_i=0 rs1_val_i=0x00000005 rs2_val_i=0x00000003 → mul_start_o=1, stall_req_o=1, wb_we_o=0
MUL_COMPLETE_WRITEBACK: is_mul_i=1 rd_i=1 mul_done_i=1 mul_result_i=0x0000000F → wb_we_o=1, wb_rd_o=1, wb_data_o=0x0000000F, stall_req_o=0
LOAD_WRITEBACK: is_load_i=1 rd_i=1 load_data_i=0x12345678 → wb_we_o=1, wb_rd_o=1, wb_data_o=0x12345678
CSR_WRITEBACK: is_csr_i=1 rd_i=1 csr_rdata_i=0x00001800 → wb_we_o=1, wb_rd_o=1, wb_data_o=0x00001800
RESET_CLEARS_OUTPUTS: rst_n=0 → wb_we_o=0, mul_start_o=0, stall_req_o=0

register_file_rv32e.description: 16 x 32 integer register file for RV32E with two read ports and one write port. x0 is hardwired to zero; x1-x15 are writable.
inferred_testcases: READ_X0_ZERO: we_i=0 raddr1_i=0 raddr2_i=0 → rdata1_o=0x00000000, rdata2_o=0x00000000
WRITE_X1_READBACK: write we_i=1 waddr_i=1 wdata_i=0x12345678 then read raddr1_i=1 raddr2_i=0 → rdata1_o=0x12345678, rdata2_o=0x00000000
WRITE_X15_READBACK: write we_i=1 waddr_i=15 wdata_i=0xA5A5A5A5 then read raddr1_i=15 raddr2_i=1 → rdata1_o=0xA5A5A5A5, rdata2_o=stored_x1_value
WRITE_X0_IGNORED: write we_i=1 waddr_i=0 wdata_i=0xFFFFFFFF then read raddr1_i=0 raddr2_i=0 → rdata1_o=0x00000000, rdata2_o=0x00000000

csr_trap_controller.description: Maintains machine-mode CSR state needed for traps and interrupt handling, detects trap causes, provides mtvec redirect, saves mepc/mcause, and handles MRET return to mepc.
inferred_testcases: RESET_CSR_INIT: rst_n=0 → trap_taken_o=0, mret_redirect_o=0, mepc_o=defined_reset_value, mcause_o=defined_reset_value
ILLEGAL_INSTR_TRAP: rst_n=1 illegal_instr_i=1 ebreak_i=0 ext_irq_i=0 pc_i=0x00000100 → trap_taken_o=1, trap_pc_o=mtvec_value, mepc_o=0x00000100, mcause_o=2
EBREAK_TRAP: rst_n=1 illegal_instr_i=0 ebreak_i=1 ext_irq_i=0 pc_i=0x00000104 → trap_taken_o=1, trap_pc_o=mtvec_value, mepc_o=0x00000104, mcause_o=3
EXTERNAL_INTERRUPT_TRAP: rst_n=1 illegal_instr_i=0 ebreak_i=0 ext_irq_i=1 pc_i=0x00000108 → trap_taken_o=1, trap_pc_o=mtvec_value, mepc_o=0x00000108, mcause_o=0x8000000B
CSR_WRITE_MTVEC: csr_we_i=1 csr_addr_i=0x305 csr_wdata_i=0x00000400 → csr_rdata_o for mtvec reads back 0x00000400
MRET_REDIRECT: after prior trap saved mepc_o=0x00000108, mret_i=1 → mret_redirect_o=1, trap_taken_o=0

load_store_unit.description: Computes effective addresses, issues load/store requests, handles byte enables, and waits for variable-latency memory responses.
inferred_testcases: LW_REQUEST: req_valid_i=1 is_store_i=0 size_i=2'b10 base_addr_i=0x00001000 offset_i=0x00000004 mem_gnt_i=1 mem_rvalid_i=0 → mem_req_o=1, mem_we_o=0, mem_addr_o=0x00001004, mem_be_o=0xF, busy_o=1
LW_RESPONSE: req_valid_i=1 is_store_i=0 size_i=2'b10 base_addr_i=0x00001000 offset_i=0x00000004 mem_gnt_i=1 mem_rvalid_i=1 mem_rdata_i=0x89ABCDEF → load_data_o=0x89ABCDEF, busy_o=0

SW_REQUEST: req_valid_i=1 is_store_i=1 size_i=2'b10 base_addr_i=0x00002000 offset_i=0x00000008 store_data_i=0x12345678 mem_gnt_i=1 → mem_req_o=1, mem_we_o=1, mem_addr_o=0x00002008, mem_be_o=0xF, mem_wdata_o=0x12345678
SB_REQUEST_BYTE0: req_valid_i=1 is_store_i=1 size_i=2'b00 base_addr_i=0x00002000 offset_i=0x00000000 store_data_i=0x000000AA mem_gnt_i=1 → mem_addr_o=0x00002000, mem_be_o=0x1, mem_wdata_o=0x000000AA

SH_REQUEST_HALFWORD: req_valid_i=1 is_store_i=1 size_i=2'b01 base_addr_i=0x00002000 offset_i=0x00000002 store_data_i=0x0000BEEF mem_gnt_i=1 → mem_addr_o=0x00002002, mem_be_o=0xC, mem_wdata_o=0xBEEF0000
WAIT_STATE_BUSY: req_valid_i=1 is_store_i=0 size_i=2'b10 base_addr_i=0x00001000 offset_i=0x00000000 mem_gnt_i=1 mem_rvalid_i=0 → busy_o=1, mem_req_o=1
RESET_IDLE: rst_n=0 → mem_req_o=0, busy_o=0

boot_rom_loader_assist.description: Boot assist logic for SPI-to-IRAM mode. Reads and validates boot header/image, copies program into IRAM, signals boot completion, and enters fail-stop on SPI/header/range/validation failure.
inferred_testcases: BOOT_START_REQUEST: rst_n=1 start_i=1 spi_rx_valid_i=0 spi_error_i=0 → spi_tx_req_o=1, boot_done_o=0, fail_stop_o=0

VALID_HEADER_ACCEPT: start_i=1, spi_rx_valid_i=1, spi_rx_data_i=valid_magic_or_version_word sequence with program_size=0x00000100 start_addr=allowed_region → fail_stop_o=0, spi_tx_req_o remains active for image fetch
IMAGE_COPY_WORD: after valid header, spi_rx_valid_i=1, spi_rx_data_i=0xDEADBEEF for next program word → iram_we_o=1, iram_wdata_o=0xDEADBEEF, iram_addr_o=expected_next_iram_addr
BOOT_SUCCESS: start_i=1, valid header and full image word sequence received, spi_error_i=0 → boot_done_o=1, fail_stop_o=0
SPI_ERROR_FAIL: start_i=1, spi_error_i=1 → fail_stop_o=1, boot_done_o=0
HEADER_MALFORMED_FAIL: start_i=1, spi_rx_valid_i=1, spi_rx_data_i=invalid_magic_or_version → fail_stop_o=1, boot_done_o=0
SIZE_RANGE_FAIL: start_i=1, valid magic/version but program_size=0x00004000 → fail_stop_o=1, boot_done_o=0
START_ADDRESS_FAIL: start_i=1, valid header but start_addr outside allowed execution region → fail_stop_o=1, boot_done_o=0
RESET_CLEARS_STATUS: rst_n=0 → boot_done_o=0, fail_stop_o=0, spi_tx_req_o=0, iram_we_o=0

payload.raw_context:
RV32EC_Zmmul Microarchitecture (Detailed)

1. Overview

This document defines a concrete microarchitectural blueprint for implementing the RV32EC_Zmmul core.

Target characteristics:

3-stage single-issue in-order pipeline

Small control logic footprint

Deterministic trap/interrupt handling

Configurable multiply unit implementation

Top-level blocks:

Fetch Unit (FU)

Decode/Control Unit (DU)

Execute/Writeback Unit (EX/WB)

Integer Register File (16 x 32)

CSR/Trap Controller

Load/Store Interface

Boot ROM Loader Assist Logic (for SPI-to-IRAM mode)

2. Pipeline Structure

Stages:

F (Fetch)

D (Decode)

X (Execute + memory access + writeback)

Pipeline goals:

One instruction issued per cycle in no-stall path

Global stall and flush controls for hazards and traps

3. Fetch Unit

3.1 Responsibilities

Maintain PC and next-PC generation

Fetch 16/32-bit instruction stream

Handle compressed instruction boundaries

Redirect fetch on branch/jump/trap

3.2 PC Selection Priority (highest first)

Trap/interrupt redirect (mtvec)

Exception return (mepc via MRET)

Taken branch/jump target from X

Sequential next PC (PC + 2 or PC + 4)

3.3 Instruction Buffering

RV32EC_Zmmul Tiny MCU Core Specification (v0.2-draft)

1. Scope

Defines a Cortex-M0-class microcontroller CPU core based on RISC-V for low-cost, low-power embedded systems.

2. Architecture Overview

Core type: 32-bit in-order MCU CPU

Core ISA: RV32EC_Zmmul

Privilege levels: M-mode only

Pipeline: 3-stage (Fetch, Decode, Execute)

Issue: Single-issue

Branch prediction: Not implemented

MMU/FPU: Not implemented

3. ISA Support

Mandatory:

RV32E

C (compressed instructions)

Zmmul (multiply-only signed integer multiply subset)

Multiply/Divide behavior:

Multiply instructions supported

Divide/remainder instructions not supported

Unsupported opcode policy:

Unsupported/illegal opcodes shall raise an illegal-instruction trap

4. Interrupt and Exception Model

RISC-V machine-level trap handling

Direct trap mode only: mtvec.MODE=0

Vectored mode: Not supported

Single external interrupt input (no priority/preemption model)

Interrupt entry latency target: 12-20 cycles (implementation dependent)

5. Bus and Memory Interface

Instruction fetch path:

Primary execution memory is internal IRAM/SRAM

External SPI ROM is used as boot image source in SPI boot mode

RV32EC_Zmmul ISA Definition

1. Profile

Supported ISA profile:

RV32E

C

Zmmul

Execution/trap policy:

Machine mode only (M-mode)

Unsupported/illegal opcode -> illegal-instruction trap

Divide/remainder instructions are not supported

Register model:

Architectural integer registers: x0..x15

Any encoding that names x16..x31 is illegal for this core profile

2. Instruction Encoding Fields

2.1 32-bit Base Formats

R-type: funct7[31:25] | rs2[24:20] | rs1[19:15] | funct3[14:12] | rd[11:7] | opcode[6:0]

I-type: imm[31:20] | rs1[19:15] | funct3[14:12] | rd[11:7] | opcode[6:0]

S-type: imm[11:5][31:25] | rs2[24:20] | rs1[19:15] | funct3[14:12] | imm[4:0][11:7] | opcode[6:0]

B-type: imm[12|10:5][31:25] | rs2[24:20] | rs1[19:15] | funct3[14:12] | imm[4:1|11][11:7] | opcode[6:0]

U-type: imm[31:12] | rd[11:7] | opcode[6:0]

J-type: imm[20|10:1|11|19:12] | rd[11:7] | opcode[6:0]

2.2 16-bit Compressed Formats

CR: funct4[15:12] | rd/rs1[11:7] | rs2[6:2] | op[1:0]

CI: funct3[15:13] | imm[12] | rd/rs1[11:7] | imm[6:2] | op[1:0]

CSS: funct3[15:13] | imm[12:7] | rs2[6:2] | op[1:0]

CIW: funct3[15:13] | imm[12:5] | rd'[4:2] | op[1:0]

Trap/interrupt redirect (mtvec)

Exception return (mepc via MRET)

Taken branch/jump target from X

Sequential next PC (PC + 2 or PC + 4)

3.3 Instruction Buffering

Recommended minimal prefetch structure:

32-bit fetch register + halfword align control

Valid bit and replay support on stalls

4. Decode and Control Unit

4.1 Responsibilities

Decode RV32E, C, Zmmul opcodes

Generate control signals for EX datapath and writeback

Classify instruction type: ALU, branch/jump, load/store, CSR, multiply, trap-return

Detect illegal instruction patterns

4.2 Immediate Generation

Support all required immediate forms for RV32E/C instructions in scope

Sign-extension performed in decode path

4.3 Register Read

Two read ports from register file (or equivalent time-multiplexed implementation)

Read in D, consume in X

5. Execute/Writeback Unit

5.1 ALU Subsystem

Required operations:

Add/subtract

Logical ops (and/or/xor)

Shifts

Compare/set operations

Branch target calculation and condition evaluation

5.2 Branch and Jump Handling

Branch resolution in X

On taken control transfer, flush younger instructions (F/D) and redirect PC

No branch prediction

5.3 Multiply Unit (Zmmul)

5.2 Branch and Jump Handling

Branch resolution in X

On taken control transfer, flush younger instructions (F/D) and redirect PC

No branch prediction

5.3 Multiply Unit (Zmmul)

Configurable implementation options:

Iterative multiplier (small area, higher latency)

Multi-cycle optimized multiplier (moderate area/performance tradeoff)

Control requirements:

mul_busy handshake to pipeline control

Stall front-end while multiply result is pending

Commit writeback when multiply completes

5.4 Divide/Remainder Handling

No divide/remainder datapath

Decode of unsupported divide/remainder opcodes triggers illegal-instruction exception path

5.5 Writeback

Single architectural writeback point at end of X

Writeback sources:

ALU result

Multiply result

Load data

CSR read result (if applicable)

6. Load/Store Path

6.1 Responsibilities

Effective address calculation in X

Issue memory transaction for load/store

Return load data for register writeback

6.2 Memory Wait-State Behavior

Variable-latency memory supported via stall/ready handshake

During pending memory response:

X stage holds instruction context

F and D are stalled

6.3 Alignment and Fault Policy

Valid magic/version

program_size < 16 KB

Start address in allowed execution region

Failure conditions (any -> fail-stop):

SPI transaction timeout/error

Header malformed

Size/range violation

Validation failure (if enabled)

11. Reset and Initialization Behavior

On reset deassertion:

PC <- reset vector (Boot ROM)

Integer registers unspecified unless explicitly zeroed by ROM/startup

CSRs initialized to defined reset values

Pipeline valid bits cleared

Outstanding memory operations cancelled/invalidated

12. Clocking and Power Intent (Microarchitectural)

Clock-gating points may include:

Multiplier when idle

Fetch pipeline when globally stalled

Optional CSR/counter blocks when disabled

WFI behavior should quiesce front-end until wake condition

13. Configurable Parameters

Recommended synthesis-time parameters:

MUL_IMPL (ITERATIVE, MCYCLE_OPT)

IRAM_BYTES (default 16384)

DRAM_BYTES (default 8192)

BOOT_ROM_BYTES_MAX (16384)

ENABLE_IMAGE_VALIDATION (0/1)

TRAP_ON_MISALIGNED (0/1)

14. Verification Guidance

Minimum verification matrix:

ISA compliance subset (RV32E, C, Zmmul)

Illegal instruction trapping (including divide/rem opcodes)

TRAP_ON_MISALIGNED (0/1)

14. Verification Guidance

Minimum verification matrix:

ISA compliance subset (RV32E, C, Zmmul)

Illegal instruction trapping (including divide/rem opcodes)

Branch flush correctness under back-to-back control transfers

Interrupt entry/exit correctness (mepc/mcause/mstatus effects)

SPI-to-IRAM boot success/failure paths

Fail-stop terminal behavior

Memory wait-state robustness (load/store stalls)

Recommended methods:

Directed tests for boot/trap edge cases

Constrained-random instruction streams with reference model comparison

Assertions for stall/flush mutual exclusion and single-commit invariants

4.4 Register-register ALU (compressed)

Instruction Format Quadrant ( op[1:0] ) Key fields C.MV CR 10 funct4=1000, rd!=x0, rs2!=x0 C.ADD CR 10 funct4=1001, rd/rs1!=x0, rs2!=x0 C.ANDI CB 01 funct3=100, funct2=10, rs1', imm C.SRLI CB 01 funct3=100, funct2=00, rs1', shamt C.SRAI CB 01 funct3=100, funct2=01, rs1', shamt C.SUB CA 01 funct6=100011, funct2=00, rd'/rs1', rs2' C.XOR CA 01 funct6=100011, funct2=01, rd'/rs1', rs2' C.OR CA 01 funct6=100011, funct2=10, rd'/rs1', rs2' C.AND CA 01 funct6=100011, funct2=11, rd'/rs1', rs2'

4.5 Breakpoint

Instruction Format Quadrant ( op[1:0] ) Key fields C.EBREAK CR 10 funct4=1001, rd/rs1=x0, rs2=x0

5. Explicitly Unsupported Instructions

All divide/remainder instructions: DIV, DIVU, REM, REMU

Any RV32I/RV32E encoding that references x16..x31

Any extension opcode not listed in this file (F/D/A/V/B/...) unless added in a future revision

6. Quick Field Reference

rd: destination register (5-bit encoding; legal values map to x0..x15)

rs1, rs2: source registers

rd', rs1', rs2': compressed compact regs (x8..x15)

imm: sign-extended immediate

uimm: zero-extended immediate

shamt: shift amount

csr: CSR address field

Optional image validation (policy is SoC/product-defined)

On boot failure (e.g., SPI read/validation failure), system enters fail-stop state

7. Toolchain

GCC RISC-V support (bare-metal)

8. Design Targets (Non-Normative)

Performance/area class: Cortex-M0-class

Priorities: low gate count, deterministic behavior, low power

9. Configurability

Multiply microarchitecture choice (e.g., iterative vs optimized multi-cycle)

SoC-defined APB map and memory sizing

10. Open Items

Final APB memory map and peripheral slot allocation

Reset/application vector addresses

Boot mode select mechanism (e.g., strap pin or configuration fuse)

Instruction/data interface timing and handshake details

Instruction-class cycle timing table

Sleep/deep-sleep behavior and WFI policy

Boot image validation policy details (if enabled)
